title: 10 best herbal capsules for viral hepatitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for viral hepatitis are dietary supplements that contain a variety of herbs and plant extracts believed to support liver health and function. These supplements may help reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and improve liver enzyme levels, all of which can be beneficial for individuals with viral hepatitis. Examples of herbs commonly found in these capsules include milk thistle, turmeric, and schisandra. For instance, milk thistle contains silymarin, a compound that has been shown to protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration. By taking herbal capsules, individuals with viral hepatitis may experience improved energy levels, reduced symptoms, and a better overall quality of life. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains active compounds that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help to reduce the replication of the hepatitis virus and decrease inflammation in the liver. Additionally, licorice has been shown to have a protective effect on liver cells, helping to prevent further damage. Furthermore, it can help to reduce symptoms such as fatigue, nausea, and abdominal pain associated with viral hepatitis. However, it is important to note that licorice should only be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as excessive consumption can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium depletion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important in viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and promote liver healing. This property is due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.

Antiviral: Licorice has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, slowing down the progression of the disease. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Licorice can protect the liver from damage caused by the hepatitis virus, which is important for maintaining liver function and preventing further complications. This property is due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.

Immunomodulatory: Licorice can help regulate the immune system, which can be beneficial in viral hepatitis as it can help the body fight off the virus more effectively. This property is due to the active constituents glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Anti-fibrotic: Licorice has been shown to have anti-fibrotic properties, which can help prevent the formation of scar tissue in the liver, a common complication of viral hepatitis. This property is due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1,000 mg of dried licorice root and 400 mg of milk thistle extract, ensuring the licorice root is cut into small pieces for easier grinding.
Combine the licorice root and milk thistle extract in a spice grinder or coffee grinder, pulsing until a fine, consistent powder is achieved.
Carefully measure out 500 mg of the resulting herbal powder and encapsulate it using size "00" empty gelatin capsules, following the manufacturer's instructions.
Store the completed herbal licorice capsules in a cool, dry, and dark place, ensuring they are properly sealed to maintain potency.
Take one capsule three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, for a total of three capsules per day, for a duration of two weeks or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains silymarin, a potent antioxidant that protects liver cells from damage and inflammation. This compound also stimulates the production of new liver cells, promoting regeneration and repair. Moreover, milk thistle has been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis C virus, making it an effective supplement for managing viral hepatitis. Its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties make milk thistle a valuable tool in supporting liver health and function in individuals with hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important in viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate liver inflammation caused by the virus. This property is attributed to the active constituent, silymarin.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle has antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which are increased in viral hepatitis. This is also due to the presence of silymarin.

Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle has a protective effect on the liver, which is crucial in viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage. The active constituent, silibinin, a flavonoid and a major component of silymarin, is responsible for this property.

Immune-modulating: Milk thistle can help regulate the immune system, which is important in viral hepatitis as it can help control the body's response to the virus. This is attributed to the active constituent, silymarin.

Antiviral: Some studies suggest that milk thistle may have antiviral properties, which could potentially help in the treatment of viral hepatitis. This is still a area of ongoing research, but initial findings suggest that silymarin may have antiviral effects against certain types of viruses, including hepatitis C virus.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried milk thistle seeds and size “00” empty vegetarian capsules.
Grind 200 grams of milk thistle seeds into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Fill each capsule with 500mg of milk thistle powder, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the finished capsules in a cool, dry place, in an airtight container, for up to 6 months.
Take 1-2 capsules, 2 times daily, as a dietary supplement for viral hepatitis, with food and water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Curcumin has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus in the body. Additionally, turmeric can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of viral hepatitis. The antioxidant properties of turmeric can also help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. Furthermore, turmeric can help stimulate the production of bile, which is essential for digestion and can support liver function. Overall, turmeric capsules can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for viral hepatitis, helping to reduce inflammation, protect liver cells, and support liver function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and promote liver healing. This property is due to the active constituent curcumin.

Antioxidant: Turmeric capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property.

Antiviral: Turmeric capsules have been shown to have antiviral properties, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The active constituents curcumin and turmeric oil have been found to have antiviral properties.

Hepatoprotective: Turmeric capsules can help protect the liver from damage and promote liver function, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help support liver health during the healing process. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this hepatoprotective effect.

Immunomodulatory: Turmeric capsules can help regulate the immune system, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent an overactive immune response that can contribute to liver damage. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this immunomodulatory effect.
remedy_recipe: Gather and measure ingredients: 5 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of black pepper, and 1 tablespoon of milk thistle extract.
Mix turmeric powder, black pepper, and milk thistle extract in a bowl until well combined.
Scoop a measured amount (e.g. 1 teaspoon) of the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler tool.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place, in an airtight container for up to 6 months.
Take 2-3 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baical skullcap capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in baical skullcap, such as baicalin and wogonin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses and reduce inflammation in the liver. This herbal remedy can help alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis, such as fatigue, nausea, and jaundice, while also promoting liver health and function. Baical skullcap capsule can be used as a complementary therapy alongside conventional treatments to manage viral hepatitis and support overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Baical skullcap can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and potentially slow the progression of the disease. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids such as baicalein and wogonin.

Antiviral: Baical skullcap has been shown to have antiviral properties, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The antiviral effects are attributed to the flavonoids and other constituents such as baicalin and wogonoside.

Hepatoprotective: Baical skullcap can protect the liver from damage, which is crucial for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver deterioration. This property is attributed to the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects of the flavonoids and other compounds in baical skullcap.

Antioxidant: Baical skullcap has strong antioxidant properties, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help reduce oxidative stress and liver cell damage caused by the virus and the immune response. The antioxidant activity is primarily due to the flavonoids, including baicalein, wogonin, and oroxylin A.

Immunomodulatory: Baical skullcap can modulate the immune response, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help balance the immune system's response to the virus and potentially reduce inflammation and liver damage. The immunomodulatory effects are attributed to the flavonoids and other constituents, such as baicalin and wogonoside.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Baical Skullcap herb and 1 teaspoon of dried Milk Thistle herb.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, for approximately 5-10 minutes.
Mix the ground herbs with 1-2 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler, until well combined.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, and let the capsules dry for 24 hours.
Store the finished Baical Skullcap and Milk Thistle capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry place, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that support liver function and aid in detoxification. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as taraxacin, taraxacerin, and inulin, have been shown to protect the liver from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. Moreover, dandelion has been traditionally used to treat various liver ailments, including hepatitis, due to its hepatoprotective and immune-boosting effects. By promoting bile production and facilitating the elimination of toxins, dandelion capsules can help alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis and support overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Antihepatotoxic: Dandelion capsules help to protect the liver from damage caused by viral hepatitis, reducing the risk of liver failure. This is due to the presence of taraxasterol and taraxacin, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects.

Antiviral: Dandelion capsules have antiviral properties that can help to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, slowing down the progression of the disease. The antiviral activity is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as taraxacin and taraxasterol.

Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the liver, alleviating symptoms such as pain and swelling. The anti-inflammatory effects are due to the presence of flavonoids, such as luteolin and quercetin.

Antioxidant: Dandelion capsules have antioxidant properties that can help to protect liver cells from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of liver damage. The antioxidant activity is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.

Diuretic: Dandelion capsules have diuretic properties that can help to eliminate toxins from the body, reducing the load on the liver. The diuretic effects are due to the presence of potassium and other minerals, which help to increase urine production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dandelion plants from a clean, pesticide-free environment, ensuring the flowers, leaves, and roots are intact; clean and dry the plants, then finely chop them.
Combine 2 parts chopped dandelion leaves, 1.5 parts flowers, and 1 part roots, grinding the mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Measure 500 mg of the ground dandelion mixture and fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers, preferably in a cool, dark place, and consume 2-3 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals.
Continue the regimen for at least three months, monitoring liver enzyme levels and overall health while consulting a healthcare professional for guidance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties make it effective in supporting liver function and reducing inflammation, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of viral hepatitis. Barberry has also been shown to have a protective effect on the liver, helping to prevent damage caused by the virus. Additionally, barberry has been found to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the digestion and absorption of fats and fat-soluble vitamins, further supporting liver function. Overall, common barberry capsule can be a helpful supplement for those looking to support their liver health and alleviate the symptoms of viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Barberry contains berberine, which has been shown to have antiviral properties and can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus.

Hepatoprotective property: Barberry has hepatoprotective effects, which means it can protect the liver from damage caused by the hepatitis virus. This is important because viral hepatitis can cause significant liver damage.

Anti-inflammatory property: Barberry has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the liver caused by the hepatitis virus. This is important because inflammation can exacerbate liver damage and lead to further complications.

Immunomodulatory property: Barberry has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune response. This is important because an overactive immune response can contribute to liver damage in individuals with viral hepatitis.

Antioxidant property: Barberry contains antioxidants, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals. This is important because viral hepatitis can lead to an increase in free radicals, which can cause further damage to liver cells.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried common barberry bark and 1 teaspoon of dried milk thistle seeds, then grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herbs with 1 teaspoon of slippery elm powder and 1 teaspoon of psyllium husk powder to help form a capsule-like consistency.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or manually.
Store the completed barberry capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.
Take 2-3 capsules, three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, for up to 3 months or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Andrographis capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains potent compounds like andrographolide, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the replication of the hepatitis virus and alleviate inflammation in the liver. Moreover, andrographis can help boost the immune system, which is crucial in fighting off viral infections. Its hepatoprotective effects can also help protect the liver from further damage caused by the virus. Therefore, andrographis capsules can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for viral hepatitis, although it is always important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Andrographis capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and prevent further damage to the liver. This property is attributed to the diterpenoid lactones, including andrographolide, which inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines.

Antiviral: Andrographis capsules have been shown to inhibit viral replication, which is crucial for viral hepatitis as it can help limit the spread of the virus within the body. The antiviral properties are attributed to the diterpenoid lactones, particularly andrographolide, which interfere with viral attachment and penetration.

Hepatoprotective: Andrographis capsules can protect the liver from damage, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further progression of the disease. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and diterpenoid lactones, which enhance liver function and protect against oxidative stress.

Immunomodulatory: Andrographis capsules can regulate the immune response, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help the body fight off the virus. This property is attributed to the diterpenoid lactones, which modulate the production of immune cells and cytokines.

Antioxidant: Andrographis capsules have antioxidant properties, which are important for viral hepatitis as they can help protect the liver from oxidative stress caused by the virus. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and diterpenoid lactones, which scavenge free radicals and reduce lipid peroxidation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 450mg of dried Andrographis paniculata leaves and a capsule-making machine.
Grind the leaves into a fine powder, passing through a 200-mesh sieve, taking approximately 20-30 minutes.
Measure 350mg of the Andrographis powder and place it into size "00" empty gelatin capsules using the capsule-making machine.
Repeat step 3 until all powder is used, producing around 150 capsules.
Store the finished Andrographis capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Katuki capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains the extract of Picrorhiza kurroa, a plant that has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries. This herb has been shown to have liver-protecting properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and damage caused by the hepatitis virus. Katuki capsule has been found to improve liver function, reduce liver enzyme levels, and protect against liver fibrosis. Additionally, it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress in the body. Overall, Katuki capsule can help to manage the symptoms of viral hepatitis and support the liver in its natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Katuki capsules contain active constituents such as picrosides and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it reduces damage to liver cells caused by the virus.

Antiviral: Katuki capsules have antiviral properties due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other compounds, helping to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, thus slowing down the progression of the disease.

Hepatoprotective: Katuki capsules have hepatoprotective properties, which means they protect the liver from damage. This is crucial for viral hepatitis as it helps to maintain liver function and prevent further liver damage caused by the virus.

Antioxidant: Katuki capsules contain antioxidants, such as flavonoids and tannins, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the liver. This is important for viral hepatitis as it helps to protect liver cells from oxidative damage caused by the virus.

Immune-boosting: Katuki capsules have immune-boosting properties due to the presence of various active constituents, including polysaccharides and triterpenes. This is important for viral hepatitis as a strong immune system can help the body to better fight off the virus and promote recovery.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500mg of Katuki powder, a well-known herb for treating liver disorders, including viral hepatitis.
Combine Katuki powder with 100mg of milk thistle and 100mg of dandelion root, both known for their liver-protecting properties.
Mix the herbal powders thoroughly and fill size 00 capsules with the mixture, resulting in approximately 20-25 capsules.
Store the Katuki capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, for a shelf life of up to 6 months.
For viral hepatitis treatment, take 1-2 capsules, twice daily, preferably after meals, or as directed by your healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stonebreaker capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains a powerful herb called Cardenchia lustianca, also known as stonebreaker or chanca piedra. This herb has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various liver and kidney ailments. It is believed to have antiviral properties that can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, thereby reducing inflammation and damage to the liver. Additionally, stonebreaker is thought to promote the flow of bile and support the liver's natural detoxification processes, which can help improve overall liver function. However, it is important to note that while stonebreaker may be helpful as a complementary therapy, it should not be used as a substitute for conventional medical treatment for viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Antihepatotoxic: Stonebreaker capsules help to protect the liver from damage caused by viral hepatitis, reducing the risk of further complications. This property is due to the presence of active constituents such as flavonoids and tannins.

Antiviral: Stonebreaker capsules have the ability to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause hepatitis. This property is attributed to the presence of phyllanthin and hypophyllanthin.

Hepatoprotective: Stonebreaker capsules help to promote the regeneration and repair of liver cells, supporting the overall health of the liver. This property is due to the presence of lignans and ellagitannins.
Anti-inflammatory: Stonebreaker capsules help to reduce inflammation in the liver, alleviating symptoms of viral hepatitis. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.

Immunomodulatory: Stonebreaker capsules help to regulate and strengthen the immune system, improving the body's ability to fight off viral infections. This property is due to the presence of alkaloids and polyphenols.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried stonebreaker herb and calculate the required dosage (1-2 grams per capsule, up to 3 times a day).
Grind the dried stonebreaker herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the stonebreaker powder using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed stonebreaker capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take the recommended dosage of stonebreaker capsules (1-2 capsules, 3 times a day) as part of a treatment plan for viral hepatitis, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower capsule helps with viral hepatitis because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that support liver function and promote healing. The herb's active constituents, including chlorogenic acid, caffeic acid, and luteolin, have been shown to protect liver cells from damage caused by viral infections and toxins. Additionally, eastern purple coneflower has been found to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the digestion and elimination of harmful substances from the body. By supporting the liver's natural detoxification processes, this herb can help reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and promote overall health and well-being in individuals with viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the liver caused by viral hepatitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polyacetylenes.

Immune-boosting: Echinacea purpurea can help enhance the immune system, which is crucial for individuals with viral hepatitis as it can help the body fight off the virus. The main active constituents responsible for this property are alkamides, polysaccharides, and glycoproteins.

Antiviral: Eastern purple coneflower has antiviral properties that can directly inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The active constituents responsible for this property include cichoric acid, echinacoside, and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Echinacea purpurea has potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect liver cells from oxidative damage caused by viral hepatitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include cichoric acid, phenolic acids, and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Eastern purple coneflower has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, which means it can protect the liver from damage caused by viral hepatitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polyacetylenes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2,000 mg of dried Echinacea purpurea (eastern purple coneflower) herb and grind it into a fine powder.
Measure out 500 mg of the ground Echinacea purpurea powder and mix it with an appropriate binder to create a homogeneous mixture.
Fill size "00" gelatin capsules with the Echinacea purpurea-binder mixture, using a capsule filling machine.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a clean, dry area away from direct sunlight.
Store the dried, filled capsules in airtight containers, in a cool, dark place, and use as directed for viral hepatitis treatment.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat viral hepatitis with herbal capsules is through the use of milk thistle, licorice root, and turmeric. These natural remedies have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce liver inflammation, protect liver cells, and improve liver function. Additionally, schisandra and reishi mushroom can help boost the immune system and support liver detoxification. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to viral hepatitis that are treated with herbal capsules include chronic liver diseases such as cirrhosis and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. Certain herbs like milk thistle, turmeric, and dandelion have been shown to support liver health and function, reduce inflammation, and improve bile production. These herbs can help protect the liver from damage, promote healing, and improve overall liver function. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, especially for those with liver disease or taking medication.
